marketState: neutral-bearish — extreme heat priced in, storage surplus and deteriorating seasonality cap medium-term upside
summary: Front month sits at $3.287, roughly $0.33 above the fair-value midpoint of $2.960 but still inside the $2.460–$3.460 band, pointing to mild overvaluation rather than an extreme stretch. Weather demand is genuinely extraordinary — the 1-7 day window is +28% above 5-year normal and already above the historical max range, while the 8-16 day window extends that to +41% above 5y max — providing credible near-term support. Against this, storage stands +151 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish injection surprise this week (+6 Bcf above forecast), and seasonal probabilities strongly favour lower prices over the next four weeks (only 19% chance of higher, typical move -1.8%). Managed money is at the 18th percentile of net positioning over 5 years (very short), creating a potential short-squeeze tail risk but also reflecting broad professional conviction that fundamentals are bearish. Futures curve data was unavailable; the small front-to-next backwardation of ~$0.05 confirms only modest near-term demand scarcity premium.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonal probabilities are strongly bearish at the 4-week horizon — only 19% chance of a higher price, typical 4-week move -1.8% — and the current cooling season peaks in August before the weak Sep-into-Jan pattern takes hold. Storage carries a 151 Bcf surplus above the 5-year average with injections running above forecast, meaning heat demand has not yet drawn the surplus down to a supply-tight level. Fair value declines over the next two weeks ($2.945–$2.960) while gradually converging back to price by day 21 ($3.152), suggesting the current premium fades as heat subsides.
longTerm.invalidation: Weekly close above $3.58 (above the current fair-value upper band of $3.46) or managed-money net position rising above the 40th percentile on the next COT report — either would signal a sentiment reversal and expose the short to a squeeze.

shortTerm.rationale: Both the 1-7 day and 8-16 day demand windows are above the 5-year historical maximum range (+28% and +41% vs 5y norm respectively), giving bulls a live fundamental catalyst. Price is only modestly above fair value (+$0.33 vs $2.960 midpoint) — not stretched enough to fade into ongoing extreme heat. Short-term technicals are conflicted: 4h is bullish with RSI 51.5 and positive MACD, while 5m and 15m are bearish/neutral with negative MACD — no clean directional edge to lean on.
shortTerm.trigger: Enter SHORT if (a) the 1-7 day demand forecast drops back inside the 5-year max range (demand_1_7D pctVs5y falls below ~12%), signalling heat peak has passed, OR (b) front price rallies to $3.44–$3.50, providing an entry near the fair-value upper band with a tighter stop above $3.60 and materially better risk/reward.

keyDrivers: Extreme heat demand: 1-7D +28% and 8-16D +41% above 5y norm, both exceeding historical max range — strongest near-term bullish factor
Storage surplus: +151 Bcf vs 5y average with a bearish injection surprise (+6 Bcf above +70 Bcf forecast) — ongoing structural headwind
Weak seasonality: only 19% probability of higher price at 4-week horizon; typical 4-week move -1.8%; reliability rated Low (51 pp spread)
Managed money at 18th percentile net short (5y) — extreme short positioning creates short-squeeze tail risk on any bullish catalyst
Fair value $2.96–$3.15 over next 3 weeks — price $0.13–$0.33 above midpoint; converges to fair by day 21
risks: Heat wave extending beyond the 8-16 day forecast window could keep demand above 5y max range and force short covering
Managed money at 18th percentile short — a single bullish catalyst (storage draw beat, hurricane threat) could trigger a rapid squeeze toward $3.60–$3.80
